changes.note: Docs-only sprint. No Intelligence Core files in scope. HIGH is conservative for a documentation sprint but not a kernel blocker. HIGH is justifiable: this strategic programme document governs sequencing of all future HIGH-risk sprints. GPT confirmation of intent is implicit in the prompt.

ADR-LAYER-BOUNDARY-RECONCILIATION-1 status is 'Accepted — pending human ratification' (ADR line 5). The output paper (§3, §6) must accurately reflect this pending status. Cursor must not describe the ADR as unconditionally adopted or use language implying full ratification without human sign-off. This is a content quality constraint, not a blocker.

Prompt allows AUTHORITY_MAP.md update only if Cursor explicitly justifies why. Default is leave unchanged. Any update must be minimal (one-line entry) and explained in the required final report. This is not a blocker — it is a constrained permission.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — all 14 required authority paths and all 10 supporting paths confirmed present. Front matter valid (all 5 fields). Two stale findings confirmed and correctly targeted in §7: (1) Claude's '6 HIGH UAT defects unresolved' is superseded by R2 which shows HIGH 6→0; (2) package count discrepancy 186 (Cursor) vs 187 (Claude) is real and must be reconciled. Two non-blocking advisories: ADR is 'Accepted — pending human ratification' and must not be overstated; AUTHORITY_MAP update is conditional and must be justified. No behavioural hardening required. Cursor may proceed.
